evals.name: clean-cluster-ready
evals.prompt:
I'm testing the EKS upgrade-check scoring logic with mock data. Do NOT run any aws or kubectl commands. Use the findings below as if you had already executed all checks.

Read `skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md` for the scoring algorithm and report template, then generate the report.

## Cluster Metadata
- Cluster: clean-prod
- Region: us-west-2
- Account: 123456789012
- Current Version: 1.31
- Target Version: 1.32
- Cluster Status: ACTIVE
- Assessment Date: 2026-05-09 15:00

## Findings from Assessment

### Version Validation (Step 1)
- Current: 1.31, Target: 1.32 — valid one-hop upgrade
- 1.31 is in EXTENDED support (ends November 26, 2026) — assessment date is before that
- Node groups all at 1.31, skew against target = 1 (within policy)

### Breaking Changes (Step 2)
- No breaking changes apply for target 1.32

### Deprecated APIs (Step 3)
- No deprecated or removed APIs found in cluster

### Add-on Compatibility (Step 4)
- vpc-cni v1.19.2: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- coredns v1.11.4: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- kube-proxy v1.31.4: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- aws-ebs-csi-driver v1.40.0: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- No OSS add-ons found
- Karpenter: not installed

### Node Readiness (Step 5)
- 1 node group: clean-prod-ng, version 1.31, AL2023, t3.large, 3/3/6 scaling
- All nodes on containerd 2.x
- No self-managed nodes
- Subnet IPs: subnet-aaa (52 available), subnet-bbb (48 available), subnet-ccc (61 available)

### Workload Risks (Step 6)
- 4 deployments in default namespace, all have: replicas >= 2, RollingUpdate, readiness+liveness probes, cpu+mem requests
- All multi-replica deployments have matching PDBs
- No DaemonSets or StatefulSets in non-system namespaces
- No drain-blocking PDBs

### AWS Upgrade Insights (Step 7)
- 4 insights, all PASSING

### AL2 / Behavioral
- No AL2 nodes
- No behavioral changes for 1.32 target

Generate the full readiness report inline in your response.
evals.expected_output: A complete readiness report with score 90-100% (READY), no blockers, all required report sections present, and an upgrade plan referencing the cluster name `clean-prod`.
expectations: The report shows a readiness score of 90 or higher and the rating READY. TODO: human review
The Blockers & Critical Actions section is empty or contains exactly the phrase 'No blockers identified'. TODO: human review

The report contains all required top-level sections in order: Readiness Score, Blockers & Critical Actions, Recommended Actions, Informational Findings, Evidence, Upgrade Plan, AWS Reference Links. TODO: human review
The Upgrade Plan references the cluster name `clean-prod`, region `us-west-2`, current version 1.31, and target version 1.32 — not generic placeholders. TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Score 90-100 maps to READY rating
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — Score Interpretation table: 90-100 = READY

knowledge_assertions.context: Clean cluster must have empty blockers section
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — report template says write 'No blockers identified.' when section is empty

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must reference the actual cluster name, not placeholders
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — report template uses [CLUSTER] placeholders that must be filled

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include the cluster region
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — report metadata table requires Region field

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must contain the Upgrade Plan section
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — required section order includes Upgrade Plan

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include the Score Breakdown table for auditability
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — Step 2 requires Score Breakdown table

knowledge_assertions.context: Upgrade Plan must include the control plane upgrade CLI command
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — Upgrade Plan Step 2 template includes aws eks update-cluster-version

evals.name: hard-blocker-karpenter
evals.prompt:
I'm testing the EKS upgrade-check scoring logic with mock data. Do NOT run any aws or kubectl commands. Use the findings below as if you had already executed all checks.

Read `skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md` for the scoring algorithm and report template, then generate the report.

## Cluster Metadata
- Cluster: payment-prod
- Region: us-east-1
- Account: 987654321098
- Current Version: 1.30
- Target Version: 1.31
- Cluster Status: ACTIVE
- Assessment Date: 2026-05-09 15:00

## Findings from Assessment

### Version Validation (Step 1)
- Current: 1.30, Target: 1.31 — valid one-hop upgrade
- 1.30 is in EXTENDED support (ends July 23, 2026) — assessment date is before that
- Node groups all at 1.30, skew against target = 1 (within policy)

### Breaking Changes (Step 2)
- No breaking changes apply for target 1.31

### Deprecated APIs (Step 3)
- No deprecated or removed APIs found in cluster

### Add-on Compatibility (Step 4)
- vpc-cni v1.20.4: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- coredns v1.11.4: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- kube-proxy v1.30.14: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- aws-ebs-csi-driver v1.53.0: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- Karpenter v0.37.0 installed — INCOMPATIBLE with K8s 1.31 (requires >= 1.0.5)

### Node Readiness (Step 5)
- 1 node group: payment-prod-ng, version 1.30, AL2023, t3.medium, 2/2/4 scaling
- All nodes on containerd 2.x
- No self-managed nodes
- Subnet IPs: subnet-aaa (35 available), subnet-bbb (42 available)

### Workload Risks (Step 6)
- 3 deployments in default namespace:
  - order-service: replicas=3, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - payment-api: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present

- No DaemonSets or StatefulSets in non-system namespaces

- 5 insights, all PASSING

### AL2 / Behavioral
- No AL2 nodes
- No behavioral changes for 1.31 target

Generate the full readiness report inline in your response.
evals.expected_output: A readiness report with score capped at <= 59% (NOT READY) due to the Karpenter hard-blocker override, Karpenter 0.37 explicitly named as the blocker, and the Upgrade Plan placing the Karpenter upgrade BEFORE the control-plane upgrade.
expectations: The readiness score is 59 or lower and the rating is NOT READY. TODO: human review
The Blockers & Critical Actions section explicitly names Karpenter as a blocker and references the installed version 0.37 (or 0.37.0) and the target K8s version 1.31. TODO: human review
The Upgrade Plan sequences the Karpenter upgrade to v1.0.5 or later before the control-plane upgrade — not after. TODO: human review
The report explains that the score cap is the result of a hard-blocker override and would otherwise have been higher based on arithmetic alone. TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Hard blocker must either cap score at 59 or below, or display an annotation that the score was overridden
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — Hard Blocker Override: score = min(score, 59)

knowledge_assertions.context: Score 0-59 maps to NOT READY rating
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — Score Interpretation table: 0-59 = NOT READY

knowledge_assertions.context: Karpenter must be named as the hard blocker
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — hard blocker #2: Karpenter version incompatible with target

knowledge_assertions.context: Must reference the installed Karpenter version 0.37
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — report must show specific findings from the assessment

knowledge_assertions.context: Must state the minimum compatible Karpenter version
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — remediation must specify the target version to upgrade to

knowledge_assertions.context: Must explain the score was capped by a hard-blocker override
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — Hard Blocker Override applies AFTER arithmetic

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must reference the actual cluster name
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — report metadata table requires Cluster field

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must contain the Upgrade Plan section
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md — required section order includes Upgrade Plan

evals.prompt:
I'm testing the EKS upgrade-check scoring logic with mock data. Do NOT run any aws or kubectl commands. Use the findings below as if you had already executed all checks.

Read `skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md` for the scoring algorithm and report template, then generate the report.

## Cluster Metadata
- Cluster: data-platform
- Region: eu-west-1
- Account: 111222333444
- Current Version: 1.31
- Target Version: 1.32
- Cluster Status: ACTIVE
- Assessment Date: 2026-05-09 15:00

## Findings from Assessment

### Version Validation (Step 1)
- Current: 1.31, Target: 1.32 — valid one-hop upgrade
- 1.31 is in EXTENDED support (ends November 26, 2026)
- Node groups all at 1.31, skew against target = 1 (within policy)

### Breaking Changes (Step 2)
- Anonymous Auth Restricted (target >= 1.32): MEDIUM severity
  - Found 1 ClusterRoleBinding granting access to system:unauthenticated for a monitoring endpoint

### Deprecated APIs (Step 3)
- flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1beta3 FlowSchema: removed in 1.32, 3 resources found — HIGH severity
- flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1beta3 PriorityLevelConfiguration: removed in 1.32, 2 resources found — HIGH severity

### Add-on Compatibility (Step 4)
- vpc-cni v1.19.0: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- coredns v1.11.4: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- kube-proxy v1.31.2: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- aws-ebs-csi-driver v1.48.0: DEGRADED — controller pods crash-looping, no IAM credentials
- Karpenter: not installed

### Node Readiness (Step 5)
- 2 node groups: data-ng-1 (1.31, AL2023, m5.xlarge, 3/3/6), data-ng-2 (1.31, AL2023, r5.2xlarge, 2/2/4)
- All nodes on containerd 2.x
- No self-managed nodes
- Subnet IPs: subnet-aaa (28 available), subnet-bbb (31 available), subnet-ccc (45 available)

### Workload Risks (Step 6)
- 6 deployments in non-system namespaces:
  - spark-driver: replicas=1, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - kafka-consumer: replicas=3, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - api-gateway: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - batch-processor: replicas=1, Recreate, no probes, no requests
  - monitoring-dashboard: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - data-ingestion: replicas=3, RollingUpdate, no probes, requests present
- PDBs exist for kafka-consumer, api-gateway, monitoring-dashboard
- No drain-blocking PDBs

### AWS Upgrade Insights (Step 7)
- 6 insights: 5 PASSING, 1 WARNING (deprecated FlowSchema APIs, flagged for 1.32)

### AL2 / Behavioral
- No AL2 nodes

Generate the full readiness report inline in your response.
evals.expected_output: A readiness report with score capped at <= 59% (NOT READY), aws-ebs-csi-driver flagged as a critical-add-on hard blocker, and IAM-credentials remediation in the upgrade plan.
expectations: The readiness score is 59 or lower and the rating is NOT READY. TODO: human review
The Blockers & Critical Actions section names aws-ebs-csi-driver (or 'EBS CSI driver') as a blocker and identifies its DEGRADED status as the trigger. TODO: human review

The blocker section includes a remediation step that addresses the IAM-credentials root cause (e.g., verify IRSA / pod identity, restore the IAM role binding) before re-running the upgrade. TODO: human review

Findings about the FlowSchema v1beta3 removal (a valid hard blocker — removed API in target version with resources found) and the Anonymous Auth restriction are present in the report alongside the DEGRADED add-on blocker. TODO: human review

evals.prompt:
I'm testing the EKS upgrade-check scoring logic with mock data. Do NOT run any aws or kubectl commands. Use the findings below as if you had already executed all checks.

Read `skills/eks-upgrade-check/references/report-generation.md` for the scoring algorithm and report template, then generate the report.

## Cluster Metadata
- Cluster: edge-services
- Region: ap-southeast-1
- Account: 555666777888
- Current Version: 1.32
- Target Version: 1.33
- Cluster Status: ACTIVE
- Assessment Date: 2026-05-09 15:00

## Findings from Assessment

### Version Validation (Step 1)
- Current: 1.32, Target: 1.33 — valid one-hop upgrade
- 1.32 is in STANDARD support (ends March 23, 2027)
- Node groups all at 1.32, skew against target = 1 (within policy)

### Breaking Changes (Step 2)
- Endpoints API Deprecated (target >= 1.33): MEDIUM severity
  - 2 custom Endpoints resources found (excluding default kubernetes endpoint)

### Deprecated APIs (Step 3)
- No removed APIs for 1.33

### Add-on Compatibility (Step 4)
- vpc-cni v1.21.0: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- coredns v1.11.6: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- kube-proxy v1.32.1: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- aws-ebs-csi-driver v1.55.0: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- Karpenter: not installed

### Node Readiness (Step 5)
- 1 node group: edge-ng, version 1.32, AL2023, t3.small, 4/4/8 scaling
- All nodes on containerd 2.x
- No self-managed nodes
- Subnet IPs: subnet-aaa (3 available), subnet-bbb (12 available)
  - subnet-aaa has < 5 IPs — HARD BLOCKER

### Workload Risks (Step 6)
- 5 deployments in non-system namespaces:
  - edge-proxy: replicas=4, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - auth-service: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - rate-limiter: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - cache-warmer: replicas=1, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - log-shipper: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, no probes, requests present
- PDBs exist for edge-proxy, auth-service, rate-limiter
- No drain-blocking PDBs

### AWS Upgrade Insights (Step 7)
- 4 insights, all PASSING

### AL2 / Behavioral
- No AL2 nodes
- No behavioral changes for 1.33 target

Generate the full readiness report inline in your response.
evals.expected_output: A readiness report with score capped at <= 59% (NOT READY), the subnet-IP-exhaustion hard-blocker named explicitly with the affected subnet ID, and remediation options to free or add IPs before upgrading.
expectations: The readiness score is 59 or lower and the rating is NOT READY. TODO: human review

The Blockers & Critical Actions section names the subnet IP exhaustion as the blocker and identifies subnet-aaa as the specific subnet with insufficient capacity (3 available, threshold is 5). TODO: human review

The blocker entry offers at least one remediation path (free unused ENIs, expand the subnet CIDR, add a new subnet) and conveys that the upgrade cannot proceed until sufficient IPs are available. TODO: human review
The report still treats subnet-bbb (12 IPs) as a non-blocker but raises it as a Recommended Action / warning given the 5-15 IP risk band. TODO: human review

evals.prompt:

## Cluster Metadata
- Cluster: staging-apps
- Region: us-east-2
- Account: 999888777666
- Current Version: 1.31
- Target Version: 1.32
- Cluster Status: ACTIVE
- Assessment Date: 2026-05-09 15:00

## Findings from Assessment

### Version Validation (Step 1)
- Current: 1.31, Target: 1.32 — valid one-hop upgrade
- 1.31 is in EXTENDED support (ends November 26, 2026)
- Node groups all at 1.31, skew against target = 1 (within policy)

### Breaking Changes (Step 2)
- Anonymous Auth Restricted (target >= 1.32): MEDIUM severity
  - Found 2 ClusterRoleBindings granting access to system:unauthenticated

### Deprecated APIs (Step 3)
- flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1beta3 FlowSchema: deprecated but still served in 1.32, 5 resources — LOW (1 API path)
- flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1beta3 PriorityLevelConfiguration: deprecated but still served in 1.32, 3 resources — LOW (1 API path)

### Add-on Compatibility (Step 4)
- vpc-cni v1.18.5: ACTIVE, UPDATE_RECOMMENDED (behind but compatible)
- coredns v1.11.4: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- kube-proxy v1.31.2: ACTIVE, COMPATIBLE
- aws-ebs-csi-driver v1.45.0: ACTIVE, UPDATE_RECOMMENDED (behind but compatible)
- external-dns v0.14.0: COMPATIBLE (verified via upstream)
- Karpenter: not installed

### Node Readiness (Step 5)
- 2 node groups: staging-ng-1 (1.31, AL2023, t3.medium, 3/3/5), staging-ng-2 (1.31, AL2023, t3.large, 2/2/4)
- All nodes on containerd 2.x
- No self-managed nodes
- Subnet IPs: subnet-aaa (22 available), subnet-bbb (19 available), subnet-ccc (31 available)

### Workload Risks (Step 6)

  - web-frontend: replicas=3, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - api-backend: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - worker-queue: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - cron-scheduler: replicas=1, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - legacy-importer: replicas=1, Recreate, no probes, no requests
  - report-generator: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, no probes, requests present
  - email-sender: replicas=2, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present
  - admin-panel: replicas=1, RollingUpdate, probes present, requests present

- No PDB for report-generator or email-sender (multi-replica without PDB)

### AL2 / Behavioral
- No AL2 nodes

Generate the full readiness report inline in your response.
evals.expected_output: A readiness report with a score in the FAIR/RISKY range (60-89), no hard-blocker override applied, and several MEDIUM-severity items aggregated under Recommended Actions — specifically the workload-risk findings, UPDATE_RECOMMENDED add-ons, and the FlowSchema deprecation warnings.
expectations: The readiness score falls between 60 and 89 (no hard-blocker override applied) and the rating is one of FAIR / RISKY / GOOD. TODO: human review

The Blockers & Critical Actions section is empty (or contains 'No blockers identified') because the FlowSchema APIs are flagged as deprecated-but-still-served (LOW), not removed-in-target. TODO: human review

The Recommended Actions section is populated with multiple MEDIUM-severity workload risks — including the single-replica + Recreate combination on `legacy-importer`, missing PDBs on `report-generator` / `email-sender`, and the UPDATE_RECOMMENDED versions for vpc-cni and aws-ebs-csi-driver. TODO: human review
The Score Breakdown table shows the workload-risks deduction at the 10-point category cap rather than the raw uncapped sum, demonstrating the per-category cap is being applied. TODO: human review
